purpose: Maps Mundell's impossible trinity onto EM energy transition financing to identify structural financing constraints and their macro consequences.
components: Energy security: reliable baseload power for economic growth
Fiscal sustainability: maintaining debt sustainability while funding transition
Transition commitments: meeting climate and deployment targets
Three financing paths: domestic fiscal expansion (→ currency weakness), external debt (→ BoP vulnerability), FDI (→ requires institutional quality)
current_assessment: Analytically sound framework. The neediest countries face the worst trilemma because FDI requires institutional quality they lack. No single financing path resolves the trilemma without creating macro vulnerabilities.
limitations: Uncorroborated by other agents beyond fx_strategist_02
Assumes rigid trilemma when partial solutions may be available
Does not account for multilateral/blended finance mechanisms
Institutional quality is hard to measure and may evolve
